name: USS Fulton (AS 11)
events: 1 Nov 1942 Around 0900 K/1, USS Sailfish (T/Cdr. J.R. Moore, USN) ended her 6th war patrol at Brisbane. At Brisbane she was initially taken in hand for a tender refit by USS Griffin (T/Capt. W.B. Throp, USN) but after about a week USS Fulton (T/Capt. E.C. Bain, USN) took over. Sailfish also spent two day in dock during which the bottom was cleaned and painted. Also one blade on the starboard propeller was renewed. (2)

21 Oct 1944 Around 1030 K/21, USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN), USS Pintado (T/Cdr. B.A. Clarey, USN) and USS Atule (T/Cdr. J.H. Maurer, USN) arrived at Saipan from Pearl Harbor. They were escorted in by USS PGM-9 (Lt. C. Morgan, USNR) which had joined around 0445 K/21.
All three submarines were taken in hand for voyage repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN).

25 Oct 1944 From 25 to 27 October 1944, USS Sailfish (T/Cdr. R.E.M. Ward, USN) and USS Parche (T/Cdr. L.P. Ramage, USN) underwent repairs alongside USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN) before they were to resume their patrols. (3)

28 Oct 1944 USS Sailfish (T/Cdr. R.E.M. Ward, USN), USS Parche (T/Cdr. L.P. Ramage, USN) and USS Pomfret (T/Lt.Cdr. J.B. Hess, USN) departed Saipan to operate as a coordinated attack group (Senior Officer T/Cdr. Ramage). USS Sailfish and USS Parche were to resume their patrols. For USS Parche this was her 3rd war patrol. They were ordered to patrol in the Luzon Strait.
On departure from Saipan, around 1200 I/28, they were escorted until around 1700 I/28 by USS Conklin (Lt.Cdr. E.L. McGibbon, USNR).
Around 1400 I/28, USS Sailfish however had to return to Saipan due to defects. Repairs were then made with the assistance of USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN). (4)
5 Nov 1944 Around 0500 I/5, USS Burrfish (T/Cdr. W.B. Perkins, Jr., USN) made rendezvous with USS Besugo (Cdr. T.L. Wogan, USN) and their escort to Saipan, USS PGM-9 (Lt. C. Morgan, USNR).
Around 1030 I/5, USS Burrfish arrived at Saipan where she was immediately taken in hand for repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN). (5)

9 Nov 1944 Around 1330 K/9, USS Pampanito (T/Capt. F.W. Fenno, Jr., USN), USS Archerfish (T/Cdr. J.F. Enright, USN) and USS Searaven (T/Lt.Cdr. R. Berthrong, USN) arrived at Saipan. They were escorted in USS Swanson (T/Lt.Cdr. W.K. Ratliff, USN) by which had joined around 0630 K/9.
The three submarines were then all taken in hand for voyage repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN). (6)
21 Nov 1944 Around 1130 I/21, USS Burrfish (T/Cdr. W.B. Perkins, Jr., USN) and her escort, USS Grayson (T/Cdr. W.V. Pratt, 2nd, USN), arrived at Saipan.
At Saipan some minor repairs were made to USS Burrfish by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN). (7)

24 Nov 1944 Around 1230 KL/24, USS Kete (T/Cdr. R.L. Rutter, USN) arrived at Saipan to repair her malfunctioning bow planes. She was taken in had for repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN).
USS Kete was escorted in by USS Fanning (T/Cdr. J.C. Bentley, USN) which had joined around 0635 KL/24.

15 Dec 1944 Around 1020 K/15, USS Spot (T/Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr., USN), USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) and USS Balao (T/Cdr. M.R. de Arellano, USN) arrived at Saipan. They were escorted in by USS PC 1127 (Lt.(jg) G.O. Pratt, USNR) which had joined around 0600 K/15.
At Saipan the submarines were taken in hand for voyage repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN). (8)

12 Jan 1945 Around 0915 K/12, USS Devilfish (T/Cdr. E.C. Stephan, USN) arrived at Saipan from Pearl Harbor. She was escorted in by USS LCI(L)-371 (Lt.(jg) B.M. Daniel, USNR) which she had made rendezvous with around 0200 K/12.
At Saipan, USS Devilfish was taken in hand for voyage repairs by the submarine tender USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN).

14 Jan 1945 Around 1130 K/14, USS Pompon (T/Cdr. S.H. Gimber, USN), USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN), USS Atule (T/Cdr. J.H. Maurer, USN) and USS Spadefish (T/Cdr. G.W. Underwood, USN) arrived at Saipan from Majuro. They were escorted in by USS PC-1126 (Lt. J.F Carrere, USNR) which had joined around 0530 K/14.

At Saipan all four submarines were taken in hand for voyage repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN). En-route to Saipan, USS Jallao had received permission to have her bow planes modified which would take several days to accomplish. She was thus unable to depart the following day with the coordinated attack group and was replaced. (9)

12 Feb 1945 Around 0300 K/12, USS Thresher (T/Cdr. J.R. Middleton, Jr., USN), USS Shad (T/Lt.Cdr. L.V. Julihn, USN), USS Peto (T/Cdr. R.H. Caldwell, Jr., USN) and USS Tilefish (T/Cdr. W.F. Schlech, Jr., USN) were joined by USS LCI(L)-1054 (Lt.(jg) W.H. Spicer, USNR) which then escorted them to Tenapag Harbor, Saipan where they arrived around 1200 K/12.
The submarines were then taken in hand for voyage repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN).
16 Mar 1945 Around 1230 I/16, USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) arrived at Saipan. She was taken in hand for voyage repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN).
She was escorted in [identity of the escort not known]. The escort had joined shortly after 0500 I/16. (10)
24 Mar 1945 At 0544 K/24, USS Devilfish (T/Lt.Cdr. S.S. Mann, Jr., USN) is joined by USS Sprangler (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Edwards, USNR) which is to escort her to Saipan.
They arrived at Saipan shortly after 1300 K/24.
USS Devilfish was then taken in hand for repairs to make her fit for onward passage to Pearl Harbor by the submarine tender USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN).

30 Mar 1945 At 0530 K/30, USS Lionfish (T/Lt.Cdr. E.D. Spruance, USN), USS Parche (T/Lt.Cdr. W.W. McCrory, USN) and USS Trutta (T/Cdr. A.C. Smith, USN) made rendezvous with USS LCI(L)-1062 (Lt.(jg) J.M. Burns, Jr., USNR) which then escorted them to Saipan where they arrived around 1230 K/30. The submarines were then taken in hand for voyage repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN).

7 Jul 1945 USS Devilfish (T/Lt.Cdr. S.S. Mann, Jr., USN) ended her 3rd war patrol at Guam. At 0405 K/7, she had made rendezvous with USS PC-596 (Lt. J.C. Bigham, Jr., USNR) which then escorted her into Apra Harbour arriving around 0730 K/7.
At Guam, USS Devilfish was taken in hand for a post patrol overhaul by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.R. St. Angelo, USN).

1 Sep 1945 The submarine tender USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.R. St. Angelo, USN) and the submarines USS Manta (T/Lt.Cdr. E.P. Madley, USN), USS Dentuda (T/Lt.Cdr. E.E. Conrad, USN), USS Argonaut (T/Cdr. J.S. Schmidt, USN) and USS Diablo (T/Cdr. G.G. Matheson, USN) departed Guam for Pearl Harbor where they arrived on 10 September 1945.
